Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: First, summarize normative flow: - Always start with Receive order. - After receiving, must check inventory before any decisions about payment authorization or fulfillment. So Check inventory must be first after Receive order. That seems clear: Check inventory must occur before Authorize payment, Package items, Ship order, Cancel order. However, Cancel order can happen after inventory check (if insufficient). So order must be: Receive order -> Check inventory. - After inventory check, two branches: 1) If inventory sufficient: Authorize payment -> Package items -> Ship order -> Close order. That is the flow. 2) If inventory insufficient: Cancel order -> Close order.

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Thus constraints: - Authorize payment must be after Check inventory and before Package items. - Package items must be after Authorize payment and before Ship order. - Ship order must be after Package items and before Close order. It should never happen after a cancellation. Also must not happen before Authorize payment (since items must be authorized before shipping). Also must not happen before Package items. Must not happen if inventory insufficient (cancellation case). - Cancel order should be after Check inventory. Should not be followed by payment authorization, packaging, shipping. Should be followed by Close order.

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Now examine each case: Case 1: Receive order -> Check inventory -> Authorize payment -> Package items -> Ship order -> Close order Sequence follows normative flow for sufficient inventory: Receive, Check, Authorize, Package, Ship, Close. This matches exactly. No violation.

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 2: Receive order -> Check inventory -> Package items -> Authorize payment -> Ship order -> Close order Here we see: Check inventory occurs before Authorize payment? Actually Authorize payment occurs after Package items, violating the rule Authorize payment should only occur after Check inventory and before packaging. The correct order: Check inventory -> Authorize payment -> Package items -> Ship -> Close. Here we have Check inventory -> Package items -> Authorize payment. So packaging before authorization. Thats a violation. Also Authorize payment after packaging violates normative constraints. Also Ship order after packaging but before payment is also a violation. So case 2 is non-compliant.

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 3: Receive order -> Check inventory -> Authorize payment -> Cancel order -> Ship order -> Close order Sequence: Check inventory, Authorize payment (makes sense if inventory sufficient). Then Cancel order (which should be after insufficient inventory; but inventory may be insufficient? Actually check inventory likely determines insufficient, but then they still authorized payment? That seems contradictory: if insufficient, they should cancel instead of authorizing payment. But they authorized payment first then canceled. According to normative, you shouldnt authorize payment before confirming inventory sufficiency. Here they did it before canceling. So the presence of both Authorize payment and Cancel order is a violation. Also after Cancel order, they then Ship order. Thats not allowed. Normative says after cancel, you should close order, not ship. So shipping after cancellation is a violation. So case 3 violates multiple constraints.

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 4: Receive order -> Check inventory -> Cancel order -> Close order Sequence: Received, Check inventory, Cancel, Close. According to normative, for insufficient inventory, you must cancel after inventory check. This matches exactly. No packaging, no payment, no shipping. This is correct. So case 4 is compliant. Case 5: Receive order -> Check inventory -> Authorize payment -> Ship order -> Close order Sequence: Received, Check inventory, Authorize payment, Ship order, Close order. Missing Package items. According to normative, packaging must occur after authorization and before shipping. Here shipping occurs directly after authorization, skipping packaging. Thats a violation. Also packaging is mandatory before shipping. So case 5 is non-compliant.

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Now need to identify which cases violate normative process: Cases 2, 3, and 5. Possibly also check that Authorize payment must be after Check inventory which holds for all except case maybe? Lets double-check each: Case 2: Check inventory at 09:30, Authorize at 10:30, thats after check (so condition satisfied). But the order between Authorize and Package is wrong. Case 3: Check inventory at 09:20, Authorize at 09:40 (after check) - okay. But then Cancel at 10:00 - after authorization. So authorize before cancel. But normative says if inventory insufficient you should cancel directly after inventory check, not authorize. So violation. Also shipping after cancel. Case 5: Check inventory at 10:00, Authorize at 10:30 - okay. Ship at 11:00 - after authorize, but missing packaging. Case 1: Perfect. Case 4: Perfect. Thus 3 cases deviate.

Name: Abductive Reasoning - IND
Text: Potential ambiguous aspects: Could payment be authorized before checking inventory? Normative says not. Could cancel happen after payment? It suggests not. Could shipping be before packaging? Not allowed. Could packaging be omitted? Not allowed; must have packaging before shipping. Could shipping occur after cancel? Not allowed. Could close order be after cancel? Yes, required. Interpretations: The normative description states that Authorize payment should only occur after Check inventory and before packaging. It also says Package items should only occur after authorization and before shipping. This implies that each step is sequential. The presence of Ship order after Cancel order violates Ship order should only occur after packaging and never after a cancellation. Potential ambiguous scenario: If inventory check reveals insufficient stock, but then they attempt to authorize payment anyway (perhaps to capture a pre-authorization hold), normative doesnt address. But the normative suggests that they shouldnt because payment authorization only makes sense once inventory sufficiency is known. So this is a violation.

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Now need to explain each violation referencing normative description and interpreting ambiguous parts if needed. Provide details. Case 2: Payment authorization after packaging violates Authorize payment must be before packaging. Case 5: Packaging missing violates Package items must be before shipping. Thus we should articulate. Additionally, perhaps case 3 also violates Cancel order must be immediate after inventory check. They canceled after authorization, which is not allowed. Now present answer with a table summarizing deviations. We also need to identify which cases violate. Provide explanation for each. Thus final answer. Will present each case and its compliance. We can also add reasoning: In case 3, because they have both payment authorization and cancellation, its ambiguous whether the inventory check indicated insufficient stock, but they still authorized payment, violating normative. So they have an unauthorized payment, and then shipping after cancellation also violates.
